Job Title: Data Engineering Specialist (Melbourne, Brisbane or Hobart location)
Ad Text:
About us

At CareSuper, we believe we’re Super Together. As a profit-to-member super fund with over 600,000 members and $60 billion in assets, we’re passionate about helping our members retire with confidence. We’re growing fast and looking for our next superhero to join us on this exciting journey.

About the role

As our new Data Engineering Specialist, you’ll be part of our Technology Delivery team. Reporting to the Manager – Enterprise Systems, you will be responsible for the design, development and optimisation of our enterprise data platform built on Azure Databricks. You will deliver scalable, cloud-native data engineering solutions in line with our Data Strategy and roadmap, enabling analytics, reporting and advanced insights across the organisation.

Please note, we are open to candidates located anywhere on the East Coast of Australia (Melbourne, Hobart, Brisbane, Sydney) for this position.

A day in the life may look like;

Providing technical and strategic advice to senior management, staff and stakeholders on Care Super’s cloud data platform, data pipelines and analytics enablement.
Designing, building and optimising data ingestion, transformation and orchestration pipelines using Azure Databricks and associated Azure services.
Troubleshooting and performance-tuning data engineering workloads, notebooks, jobs and supporting services to ensure reliability and scalability.
Producing and reviewing technical documentation, solution designs and data models, and analysing business data sources to support analytical and operational use cases.
Providing guidance and support in the use and development of modern analytics and data engineering tools, enabling self-service analytics where appropriate.
Completing escalated Level 3 data platform requests and supporting the Insights and Analytics team to resolve complex data issues and queries.
What we’re looking for

Our ideal candidate will bring strong experience designing and delivering cloud-based data engineering solutions, with a focus on Azure and Databricks. You’ll also bring the following skills:

Strong SQL and data modelling skills across relational and analytical data stores
Data ingestion, transformation and orchestration using Azure Databricks (Spark, SQL, Python/Scala)
Experience with Delta Lake, medallion architecture and modern data platform design patterns
Integration with Azure services such as Azure Data Lake Storage, Azure Data Factory, Azure Synapse and Power BI
Knowledge of Agile development methodologies and best practices
CI/CD practices for data platforms (e.g. Azure DevOps, Git-based workflows)
Experience supporting analytics, dashboards and KPIs using Databricks or Power BI
Strong documentation, stakeholder engagement and problem-solving skills
Why CareSuper?

We’re not just another super fund. We’re a values-driven organisation that puts people first—our members and our team. Here’s what you can look forward to:

Hybrid and flexible work environment
3% Superannuation Guarantee (SG) above industry minimum
Matching an additional superannuation salary sacrifice of up to 2%
5 weeks of annual leave
17 weeks of parental leave for all eligible employees
Holistic health and wellbeing support - free annual flu shots, skin checks, Employee Assistance Program
External Employee Coaching Program and Professional Development
Rewards and Recognition Program, as well as employee discounts and cashback offers on a broad range of products and services
Ready to apply?

We’d love to hear from you! Just send through your resume and a cover letter. Be quick—we may close applications early if we find the right person.

What happens next?

Need support during the application or interview process? We’re here to help. Just reach out to your talent partner and let us know how we can make the process work for you.

We’re committed to protecting your privacy—your personal information will be handled with care and in line with our Privacy Policy.

At CareSuper, we know that diverse perspectives make us stronger. We’re proud to foster an inclusive environment where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued—no matter your background or identity.

The role

Mansell Taylor Consulting is proud to partner with Transmax to appoint a Head of Quality & Delivery Excellence, responsible for improving engineering quality, delivery flow, and release governance across the organisation.

This is a senior leadership role focused on enabling consistent, high-quality, and predictable software delivery.

The opportunity

You will lead Quality Engineering and Agile delivery capability, embedding modern testing practices, delivery frameworks, and supporting delivery excellence and cross-cutting initiatives across all our product, platform, and operational teams.

This role plays a key part in strengthening engineering performance, improving delivery predictability, and supporting safe, scalable releases, including being a critical part of our AI-enabled process across our SDLC.

What you’ll be doing

Lead Quality Engineering and Scrum Master capability across teams
Define and implement testing strategies and automation frameworks
Improve delivery flow using Agile practices and performance metrics
Establish and govern release and change management processes
Embed modern delivery practices (CI/CD, trunk-based development)
Partner with SRE and Architecture on resilience and NFR alignment
Drive delivery metrics, reporting, and continuous improvement
Lead release governance and approval processes
Enable SDLC standards and engineering capability uplift

About you

Proven leadership in quality engineering or software delivery environments
Strong background in Agile, testing automation, and SDLC practices, understanding where the theory meets pragmatism to deliver measurable outcomes across the way people work in a software business
Experience implementing modern testing frameworks (unit, integration, E2E, performance)
Strong understanding of release governance and delivery risk
Experience in regulated or mission-critical environments
Strong stakeholder engagement across technical and business teams
Leadership experience across multidisciplinary teams
Tertiary qualification in IT, Software Engineering, or equivalent experience

Why apply

Lead delivery quality and engineering capability at scale
Shape modern software delivery practices
High-impact role across product and platform teams
Work closely with senior technology leadership

Apply

Apply now or contact Jelissa Taylor on 0415 430 303. Applications close Friday 17 April.

Category: Commercial Sales, Leasing & Property Mgmt (Real Estate & Property)
Ad Text:
Abacus is seeking an experienced Senior Portfolio Manager to play a critical role in maximising returns from a national commercial property portfolio. Working closely with the General Manager – Commercial Portfolio, this role is responsible for driving long‑term asset value, optimising income and expenditure, strengthening customer relationships, and supporting the broader Commercial team through strategic leadership and expertise.




About Abacus

Abacus Group is a strong asset-backed, annuity style business model where capital is directed towards assets that provide potential for enhanced growth and ultimately create value. Our people, market insight and repositioning capability together with strategic partnering are the key enablers of our strategy.




About the role

This is a senior asset management role with a strong focus on strategy, value creation, customer centricity and consistent service delivery across a diverse commercial portfolio.

Your key responsibilities will include:

Develop and deliver portfolio and asset management strategies to maximise income, value and investment returns.

Lead medium‑ to long‑term asset planning, including capital deployment, income optimisation, expense management and risk mitigation.

Undertake strategic asset and market analysis to ensure assets are competitively positioned.

Drive portfolio performance through leasing strategy, including appointment and management of external leasing agents and direct lease negotiations.

Build strong, trusted relationships with customers, investors and key external stakeholders.

Partner with Property and Facility Managers to ensure excellence in asset operations and tenant experience.

Manage income, operating and capital budgets in collaboration with Finance and internal stakeholders.

Contribute to high‑quality Board and internal reporting and strategic business planning.

Mentor and coach Portfolio Managers and Analysts, providing leadership across strategic asset management practices.

About you

You are a commercially minded property professional with a strong strategic lens and a passion for value creation. You bring credibility, gravitas and a proactive, ownership‑driven approach to your work.

To be successful in this role you will ideally have:

A minimum of 7 years’ experience in commercial portfolio or asset management.

Proven experience delivering long‑term asset strategies and strong financial outcomes.

Tertiary qualifications in Property or a related discipline.

Strong financial acumen, analytical capability and commercial judgement.

Excellent negotiation, deal‑making and relationship management skills.

A customer‑centric mindset with a strong focus on service quality and tenant experience.

High‑level written and verbal communication skills, with experience preparing Board‑level reporting.

The ability to work autonomously while collaborating effectively across a high‑performing team.

You’ll be part of a forward‑thinking commercial property business that values entrepreneurship, accountability and teamwork. This role offers the opportunity to influence portfolio strategy at a senior level, work with high‑quality assets and stakeholders, and make a meaningful impact on long‑term investment performance. In return, you will be rewarded with a supportive and engaging work environment with a focus on your development in the property sector.

Successful candidates will also need to complete Abacus vetting processes including reference and police checks. 

To apply please click the “Apply” button below.

Deputy is a global SaaS workforce management company with hubs in Sydney, Melbourne, San Francisco and London, plus team members working remotely across the United States. Our platform serves over 1.5 million workers and 375,000 workplaces across 100+ countries. We are backed by top global investors and recently achieved Unicorn status. 

At Deputy, we’re improving the world of work, one shift at a time, for 80% of the world’s workforce: hourly workers. These are the dedicated employees who keep our world running – from baristas to nurses, cleaners to delivery drivers, florists to factory workers. Despite their vital role in society, most workplace technology has focused on those workers who sit behind a desk, but at Deputy, we transform the frontline. When businesses use Deputy, their workplaces thrive – the business is more profitable, compliant, and productive, while the workers are more engaged and happier at work.

We're becoming an AI-native company, a commitment that means you'll be empowered (and expected) to use AI tools and thinking in your day-to-day work. You'll have the training, support, and freedom to use AI responsibly and creatively to spark ideas, solve problems faster, and unlock new ways of working.

If you’re passionate about creating solutions that put people first and helping businesses and their teams thrive, join us at Deputy and make an impact where it matters most!

We're looking for an experienced Director, Revenue Operations, Systems & Enablement to help support and scale the Commercial organization (Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, Support, Partnerships). The candidate must have a strong foundation in business operations, technical systems architecture, and team enablement. You will not only drive operational strategy and board-level reporting but also own the technical roadmap for our business systems (including billing) and ensure our GTM teams are trained and equipped to sell effectively.

This is a great opportunity to create scalable processes, build a robust technical foundation, and foster a culture of continuous learning in a fast-growing company. The role will report to the Commercial Chief of Staff.

Responsibilities:

Global Revenue Operations: Develop and execute Global Revenue Operations by collaborating with Sales, CS, and Marketing Leadership to drive key strategic initiatives.

Enablement Strategy: Own the end-to-end enablement strategy, including onboarding, continuous learning, product training, and sales process methodologies for all GTM teams.

Systems Architecture & Billing: Lead the strategic design and technical architecture of the business systems stack, serving as the owner for billing technology (Zuora) and its integration with CRM (Salesforce) and ERP systems.

Process Optimization: Optimize sales and billing processes across Quote-to-Cash, Deal Desk, Forecasting, Commissions, and GTM Strategy to ensure seamless execution.

Board & Executive Reporting: Assist CCO in design, creation, and execution of board communications/decks and weekly/quarterly business reviews.

Quality & Consistency: Proactively monitor and maintain high levels of data quality, accuracy, and process consistency across the Revenue organization.

Metrics & Dashboards: Develop key performance metrics, dashboards, and reports that help the GTM organization focus on performance drivers and results.

Cross-Functional Planning: Partner with Finance to drive annual and long-term planning, budgeting, business KPIs, and process alignment.

Key Areas of Focus & Collaboration:

STRATEGY & ENABLEMENT:

GTM Enablement: Design and deliver comprehensive training programs for Sales, CS, and Partnerships, covering product launches, sales methodology, and systems adoption.

Onboarding: Build and manage a best-in-class onboarding program to reduce ramp time for new commercial hires.

Content & Playbooks: Partner with Product Marketing to ensure GTM teams are equipped with up-to-date content, playbooks, and competitive intelligence.

Strategic Planning: Collaborate with Finance and CCO on org structure, sales strategy, and long-term planning cycles.

BUSINESS SYSTEMS & ARCHITECTURE:

Billing Infrastructure (Zuora): Own the technical implementation and management of Zuora, ensuring accurate billing, subscription management, and revenue recognition compliance.

Technical Architecture: Design and maintain a scalable GTM systems architecture, ensuring seamless data flow between Salesforce, Zuora, Marketing Automation (Marketo, Customer.io), and other tools.

Integrations: Oversee system integrations and API workflows to support a low-friction, automated Quote-to-Cash process.

Tool Management: Manage vendor selection, procurement, and optimization for the entire GTM tech stack.

PROCESS & OPERATIONS:

Quote-to-Cash: Streamline the end-to-end deal lifecycle, from CPQ to billing and revenue recognition.

Sales Process: Define and enforce policies for Deal Desk, forecasting, and pipeline management.

Operational Cadence: Drive the WW Sales and Marketing cadence (e.g., All Hands, WBRs, QBRs, SKO).

PLANNING & METRICS:

Analytics: drive deep analysis of sales capacity, territory segmentation, and funnel performance.

Compensation: Manage compensation strategy, design, compliance, and production.

You Have:

10+ years of relevant experience in a software business, with a focus on Revenue Operations, Systems, or Enablement.

Deep technical expertise in Business Systems, specifically Zuora (Billing) and Salesforce (SFDC). Experience with architecture and integration (middleware, APIs) is highly preferred.

Proven experience building or managing GTM Enablement functions, including creating curriculum, training sales teams, and managing content.

Excellent quantitative analytical skills and a keen business sense to drive decision-making.

Ability to communicate complex technical and operational concepts to executive leadership.

Experience managing cross-functional projects that touch Finance, Product, and Sales.

Ability to coach, motivate, and recruit a high-performing operations and systems team.

You share our values and work in accordance with those values.

Job Title: Strategy and Operations Lead, Regulation, AUNZ (Fixed-Term Contract)
Ad Text:
This is a fixed-term contract for a duration of 12 months.

At Google, we have a vision of empowerment and equitable opportunity for all Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples and commit to building reconciliation through Google’s technology, platforms and people and we welcome Indigenous applicants. Please see our Reconciliation Action Plan for more information.

Minimum qualifications:

Bachelor's degree or equivalent practical experience.
6 years of experience with go-to-market strategy, sales operations, strategy and operations, management consulting, finance, or program management.
Preferred qualifications:

Experience in working with strategy in ambiguous settings, with strategy implementation.
Knowledge of the Australian regulatory and policy environment.
Excellent executive presence and collaboration skills.
Excellent business acumen and judgement.
Excellent problem-solving skills with the ability to present problem statements, perform data analysis and investigations, and provide recommendations that drive commercial value.
About the job
The Go-to-Market Operations (GTM) team ensures Google's business runs smoothly by aligning priorities with execution.


In this role, you will act as a key business partner to the Country Manager, driving sustainable growth through process improvements and data-motivated insights. You will problem solving and qualified strategist and project manager, capable of providing clarity and building relationships across local and global cross-functional teams to deliver business outcomes. You will develop strategies that support leadership decision making, cross-functional alignment of goals and expectations, and help teams to execute plans. You will develop and drive business strategy in collaboration with the executive business partners, acting as a trusted advisor, prioritiser and direction setter. You will help the organization navigate internal complexities, external factors, and collaborate with cross-functional teams to set strategy, solve problems, and design, manage, and deliver projects. You will help to set the focus, pace and direction of the business across teams to deliver on business opportunities by advocating, testing, and enabling the cross-functional teams. You will focus on partnering with the cross-functional Australia and New Zealand (AUNZ) leadership, particularly around regulatory topics.


Responsibilities

Develop cross-functional plans, and deliver insights and recommendations that shape the AUNZ operating strategy. Define and solve problems and opportunities that impact the corporate reputation.
Partner with the Google AUNZ leadership team and global stakeholders to provide guidance and coordination on regulatory topics.
Establish and maintain business cadences and internal stakeholder relationships to deliver together on priorities and best practices.
Develop external intelligence and insights in the market, to enable cross-functional decision-making. Collaborate with executive stakeholders through strategy development and program management.
Lead planning and execution for the AUNZ business, collaborating with the cross-functional leadership team to identify and prioritize key focus areas. Enhance initiatives by incorporating program management best practices, data-motivated insights, and executive communication strategies.
Google is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. We also consider qualified applicants regardless of criminal histories, consistent with legal requirements. See also Google's EEO Policy and EEO is the Law. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please let us know by completing our Accommodations for Applicants form.

Are you a proactive Change Analyst looking to make a tangible impact within a complex, large-scale operational environment? This 6-month initial contract offers a unique opportunity to lead critical change activities and drive organisational readiness for a major program of work. You will join a team focused on delivering sustainable, efficient solutions while navigating a sophisticated, industrially sensitive landscape. With a strong likelihood of extension, this role is perfect for a specialist who thrives on building stakeholder buy-in and translating high-level requirements into successful business outcomes.

What You Will Do:
Deliver end-to-end change activities including assessment, analysis, and documentation to ensure the successful implementation of program objectives.
Conduct comprehensive change impact assessments using established methodologies such as PROSCI/ADKAR to support management activities.
Identify, analyse, and prepare risk mitigation tactics while proactively identifying and managing anticipated resistance to change.
Collaborate with HR, IR, LD, Communications, and other operational business groups to deliver an integrated people change service.
Build and maintain robust relationships with internal and external stakeholders to enhance project delivery and resolve engagement issues.
What You Need to Apply for This Role:
Relevant tertiary qualification or equivalent professional experience in change management.
Proven experience managing change within a highly industrialised or complex operational landscape.
Demonstrated ability to drive project milestones and deadlines while balancing the diverse needs of multiple stakeholder groups.
Strong proficiency in change management methodologies and the ability to coach or mentor team members in these practices.
Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain complex concepts clearly and write fluently for diverse audiences.
Randstad contractor benefits:
Access to a Udemy license for ongoing learning and continuous professional development
Access to exclusive discounts across various platforms
Discounts on private health funds
3 payruns a week

Interested? Apply Now!

If you are a Change Analyst with experience in a highly industrialised landscape, we want to hear from you. Click "Apply Now".

You must be a Sydney based candidate with full working rights to work in Australia to be considered.

At Randstad, we are passionate about providing equal employment opportunities and embracing diversity to the benefit of all. We actively encourage applications from any background.

Senior Data Quality Analyst | Banking | Sydney CBD

We are seeking a Senior Data Quality Analyst to join a high-profile, large-scale data migration program for a leading bank. This is a hands-on execution role focusing on customer data, product arrangements, and complex validation within a Product Model framework.

Location: Sydney CBD (Hybrid - 3 days in office) | Permanent Role

The Role

Working closely with the Data Quality Delivery Lead, you will ensure the integrity of data moving through a complex migration lifecycle. You'll be the bridge between Business, Tech, and Operations to ensure data is "fit for purpose."

Data Validation: Perform individual record validation and volumetric analysis on customer and banking product data.

Technical Execution: Drive data profiling, defect triage, and remediation tracking.

Coordination: Manage technology release activities and ensure evidence is produced for migration milestones.

Governance: Develop DQ rules, validation evidence, and reporting packs for executive stakeholders.

Technical Toolkit

SQL & Python: Strong capability for data manipulation and analysis.

Visualization: Power BI for DQ reporting and dashboards.

Cloud: Experience working within Azure Cloud environments.

Tooling: Informatica Data Quality (IDQ) is highly regarded (nice to have).

Your Background

Banking Experience: Deep understanding of banking products, customer data, and regulated environments is a significant plus.

Migration Expertise: Proven track record in large-scale data migration or transformation programs.

Data Modeling: Familiarity with Common Product Models and complex product arrangements.

Delivery Focus: Comfortable navigating ambiguity and working in a fast-paced, structured delivery environment.


Let's connect if this role aligns well with you !! Click Apply !!
